decisions.briefing: Markets pulled back modestly today with the S&P 500 down 0.7% and NASDAQ down 0.9%, a mild risk-off session. With only $298.25 in cash — well below the $500 minimum threshold — no new buys are possible today despite having 3 open slots. Holding all 7 current positions; the portfolio's +30% overall P&L is strong and the automated stop-loss and profit targets are handling downside protection.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities retreated modestly on June 3, with the S&P 500 off 0.7% and the NASDAQ sliding 0.9%, reflecting cautious investor sentiment as markets digested recent macro data. The tech-heavy NASDAQ underperformed the broader index, a typical pattern when investors rotate out of high-growth names into more defensive positions. Overall market tone was classified as neutral, suggesting this is a consolidation dip rather than the start of a broader trend reversal.
market_education.summary_zh: 美股6月3日小幅回落，標普500指數跌0.7%，以科技股為主的納斯達克指數下跌0.9%，反映市場在消化近期宏觀數據後情緒趨於審慎。科技板塊跑輸大盤，符合資金從高增長股輪動至防禦性板塊的慣常模式。整體市場方向維持中性，顯示此次調整屬技術性整固，而非趨勢性反轉的開端。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Cash drag is when idle cash sitting in a portfolio earns far less than invested assets, pulling down overall returns. Today the situation flipped — with only $298 available, the portfolio can't act on high-conviction picks like TPL or MDGL even though 3 position slots are open. Maintaining adequate 'dry powder' (reserved cash) is a key discipline in active portfolio management so you can strike when opportunities arise.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「現金拖累」是指投資組合中閒置現金的回報遠低於已投資資產，從而拖低整體收益。今日情況恰恰相反——帳戶僅剩$298現金，即便仍有3個空缺倉位，也無力部署至TPL或MDGL等高確信度標的。在主動投資管理中，保留充裕的「乾糧」（預備現金）是重要紀律，確保機會出現時能即時出手。
